allies.rationale_text: Critical-thinking gives curiosity a disciplined way to evaluate evidence, notice bias, and compare perspectives rather than just asking more questions.
allies.source_quote: Curiosity provides the drive for critical thinking.

allies.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking channels curiosity into fundamental questions that strip away assumptions and expose the elemental truths underneath the current frame.
allies.source_quote: Curiosity drives the continuous process of asking fundamental questions that strip away assumptions to arrive at the elemental truths.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Curiosity conflicts with confirmation-bias when evidence-seeking narrows into belief-protecting search patterns.
structured_tensions.source_quote: curiosity vs confirmation-bias: curiosity conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when evidence-seeking narrows into belief-protecting search patterns.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Curiosity conflicts with scaffolding when uncontrolled exploration outruns the learning structure needed for cumulative mastery.
structured_tensions.source_quote: curiosity vs scaffolding: curiosity conflicts with scaffolding (scaffolding) when uncontrolled exploration outruns the learning structure needed for cumulative mastery.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Curiosity conflicts with cognitive-load-theory when unstructured exploration exhausts working memory before learning can consolidate into durable schemas.
structured_tensions.source_quote: curiosity vs cognitive-load-theory: curiosity conflicts with cognitive-load-theory (cognitive load theory) when unstructured exploration exhausts working memory before learning can consolidate into durable schemas.

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on frame-changing inquiry plus an explicit tension block around belief protection, unstructured exploration, and overload.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader empathy or relationship relation sprawl beyond the cleanest current-vocabulary inquiry links.
open_questions: The logic-tree ally is source-real, but the first-order surface is cleaner around critical-thinking and first-principles-thinking.
